notes: Pareto evidence showed zero fragmentation and zero collisions with name-only in the evaluated population.
This is a label-based join pending availability/export of definition-bearing identity fields for phase filters; treat as provisional.
Policy is provisional pending broader samples; current Pareto evidence shows zero fragmentation and zero collisions for both phase.name and phase.seq.
phase.name is treated as the definition-bearing identifier in current evidence; phase.seq may be added only if future datasets exhibit name collisions.
Identity is definition-based and fully determined by units.spec in Phase-2 evidence.
No collisions or fragmentation observed with units.spec alone.
Policy is strong but provisional pending validation on broader populations.
Identity is definition-based via ordered segment (kind,length) sequence; segments are collapsed into line_pattern.sequence_hash.
line_pattern.sequence_hash must be derived only from segment kinds and lengths in order and must not incorporate UID, name, or element_id.
Names are labels only and must not participate in joins.
Identity is definition-based: line style path plus referenced line pattern definition via pattern_ref.sig_hash.
pattern_ref.kind is too coarse and produces high collisions; it must not be used for joins.
Definition-based identity is structural: ordered grid parameter sequence; reduce to a single derived grids_def_hash for governance joins.
Sparse per-grid scalar keys (e.g., grid[i].shift/angle) are not acceptable for identity due to high collision evidence in Phase-2 Pareto exploration.
target_id/is_solid/grid_count are context/disambiguation only and must not replace grids_def_hash.
Definition-based identity only; no referential identifiers in joins.
arrowhead.name is label-only and may be null for built-in/system arrowheads.
Downstream domains reference arrowhead identity via this domain signature, not by id or name.
Definition-based tick mark identity must be provided via referenced arrowheads domain signature (dim_type.tick_mark_sig_hash).
UID-based tick mark fields must never participate in joins, even if normalized.
Name is label-only and must not participate in joins.
Policy: color_rgb is definition-bearing.
Policy: arrowhead is definition-bearing only via arrowheads-domain signature hash (text_type.leader_arrowhead_sig_hash), not uid/name/id.
If text_type.leader_arrowhead_sig_hash is unavailable for some records, treat as lower confidence rather than substituting uid/name.
Definition-based identity is the ordered sequence of rule atoms plus logic_root and categories.
vf.rules[].sig means: include vf.rule[###].sig for indices 000..(vf.rule_count-1) in order (variable-length ordered sequence).
vf.rule[###].sig canonically binds vf.rule[###].kind + vf.rule[###].op + vf.rule[###].param_ref.id + vf.rule[###].value into a single atomic identity unit.
Names/labels and any UID-like identifiers are prohibited from joins for standards governance.
Identity is definition-based: ordered filter-stack composition via referenced filter_sig_hash values.
vfa.stack[].filter_sig_hash means: include vfa.stack[###].filter_sig_hash for indices 000..(vfa.filter_stack_count-1) in order (variable-length ordered sequence).
Template identifiers are linkage/label only and must not participate in joins.
Enabled flags are behavioral modifiers and are excluded from join identity for standards governance.
Identity is category-scoped via obj_style.row_key.
Definition includes referenced pattern via obj_style.pattern_ref.sig_hash.
obj_style.weight.projection is required to eliminate observed collisions under row_key + pattern_ref.sig_hash.
